channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Early childhood sector rallies behind 2026 Vinnies CEO Sleepout
item.description: Early childhood leaders are calling on the sector to stand together in support of the 2026 Vinnies CEO Sleepout, using the national campaign to raise awareness, challenge misconceptions about homelessness and support women and children escaping violence.
item.content:encoded: Early childhood leaders are calling on the sector to stand together in support of the 2026 Vinnies CEO Sleepout, using the national campaign to raise awareness, challenge misconceptions about homelessness and support women and children escaping violence.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: New study highlights potential role of play-based intervention in supporting toddlers with language delay
item.description: New research published in Frontiers in Rehabilitation Sciences has highlighted the potential benefits of specialist-mediated play therapy as an early intervention strategy for toddlers experiencing mild language developmental delay.
item.content:encoded: New research published in Frontiers in Rehabilitation Sciences has highlighted the potential benefits of specialist-mediated play therapy as an early intervention strategy for toddlers experiencing mild language developmental delay.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Direct payments or childcare subsidies? New policy proposal reignites debate about parental choice and affordability
item.description: A new policy proposal suggesting families could be better supported through direct cash payments rather than continued expansion of childcare subsidies has reignited debate about affordability, parental choice and the future direction of Australia’s early childhood policy settings.
item.content:encoded: A new policy proposal suggesting families could be better supported through direct cash payments rather than continued expansion of childcare subsidies has reignited debate about affordability, parental choice and the future direction of Australia’s early childhood policy settings.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Childcare centre refusal sparks governance debate over planning decision-making and transparency
item.description: A Victorian council decision to refuse a proposed childcare centre despite officer recommendations for approval has sparked broader discussion about governance, transparency and decision-making processes in the planning and early childhood sectors.
item.content:encoded: A Victorian council decision to refuse a proposed childcare centre despite officer recommendations for approval has sparked broader discussion about governance, transparency and decision-making processes in the planning and early childhood sectors.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: AI and ECEC: what conversations do providers need to consider
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is emerging as a new operational tool for some early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers, with proponents suggesting it may help reduce administrative workload, improve communication and support documentation processes.
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence (AI) is emerging as a new operational tool for some early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers, with proponents suggesting it may help reduce administrative workload, improve communication and support documentation processes.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Staff retention starts with your induction program
item.description: The Fair Work Commission's latest wage decision has delivered a pay increase for millions of award-reliant workers while drawing renewed attention to the workforce challenges facing female-dominated sectors such as early childhood education and care.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission's latest wage decision has delivered a pay increase for millions of award-reliant workers while drawing renewed attention to the workforce challenges facing female-dominated sectors such as early childhood education and care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: How Rise and Shine Kindergarten and WESHOL are bringing hands-on STEM learning to life
item.description: Rise and Shine Kindergarten has announced its partnership with We Support Hands-On Learning (WESHOL), strengthening a shared commitment to curiosity-led, inquiry-driven and hands-on learning experiences that help children build confidence, creativity and critical thinking from the earliest years.
item.content:encoded: Rise and Shine Kindergarten has announced its partnership with We Support Hands-On Learning (WESHOL), strengthening a shared commitment to curiosity-led, inquiry-driven and hands-on learning experiences that help children build confidence, creativity and critical thinking from the earliest years.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Annual Wage Review 2026 decision to be live streamed on 2 June
item.description: The Fair Work Commission will hand down its Annual Wage Review 2026 decision at 10am AEST on Tuesday, 2 June 2026, with the announcement to be live streamed nationally.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission will hand down its Annual Wage Review 2026 decision at 10am AEST on Tuesday, 2 June 2026, with the announcement to be live streamed nationally.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Writer, Podcaster and TV Presenter, Marlee Silva to be 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador
item.description: SNAICC welcomes Marlee Silva as the 2026 National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC welcomes Marlee Silva as the 2026 National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 29 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC - The Sector
item.content:encoded: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby Donations Update

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 22 May 2026

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
